title: Company Secretary
description: A Company Secretary in India is responsible for managing a company's statutory and regulatory compliance, corporate governance, and secretarial functions. They handle tasks such as filing documents, conducting board meetings, and maintaining company records. Their day-to-day work involves reviewing and implementing company policies, advising on legal matters, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.
entrance_exams: Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI) Entrance Exam
degree_name: Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com.) in Company Secretaryship
top_private_colleges: Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI)

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist in secretarial functions, conduct research, and assist in corporate governance.
career_progression.year_3_5: Take on more responsibilities, handle company secretarial functions, and advise on legal matters.
career_progression.year_6_10: Lead teams, handle complex corporate governance issues, and advise on strategic matters.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a consulting firm or a company providing corporate governance services.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities exist in the UK, US, and Singapore for Indians with experience in Company Secretaryship.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Challenging to find internships and jobs, requires strong networking.
